question_text: A loudspeaker produces sound at two different frequencies: 100 Hz and 1000 Hz. The speed of sound is 330 m/s. Which statement best explains why the 100 Hz sound travels further around corners than the 1000 Hz sound?

options.label: A
options.text: The 100 Hz sound has a longer wavelength so it diffracts more when passing around obstacles.

options.label: B
options.text: The 100 Hz sound has lower frequency so it moves slower through air.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1000 Hz sound has a shorter wavelength so it has more energy and is absorbed by obstacles.

options.label: D
options.text: The 100 Hz sound is louder so it can penetrate barriers better than the 1000 Hz sound.

explanation: Diffraction occurs when waves bend around obstacles; longer wavelengths diffract more noticeably than shorter ones. The 100 Hz sound has wavelength 330/100 = 3.3 m, while 1000 Hz has wavelength 330/1000 = 0.33 m, so the 100 Hz sound bends around corners much more easily.
